questions.question: Which ancient Indian text is primarily a collection of hymns dedicated to various deities?
questions.explanation: The Vedas are ancient Indian texts consisting mainly of hymns and are foundational to Indian spirituality and religion.

questions.question: The construction of the Qutub Minar was commissioned by which ruler?
questions.explanation: Qutb-ud-din Aibak initiated the construction of Qutub Minar in Delhi, which was completed by his successor Iltutmish.

questions.question: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched in 1920 under Mahatma Gandhi’s leadership to resist British rule through non-violent means.

questions.question: Who among the following leaders played a key role in the Champaran Satyagraha?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Champaran Satyagraha in 1917 to protest against indigo farmers' exploitation.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.explanation: The Ganges River is the longest river in India, flowing through northern India and Bangladesh.

questions.question: The Thar Desert is primarily located in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert is mainly situated in Rajasthan, covering a significant part of the state.

questions.question: Which resource is most abundant in the state of Jharkhand?
questions.explanation: Jharkhand is rich in coal deposits, making it a major coal-producing state.

questions.question: Which climate zone is predominant in the western parts of India?
questions.explanation: The western parts of India, such as Rajasthan, feature an arid desert climate.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu is the current President of India, since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: The total number of members in the Lok Sabha is:
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha has 545 members, including 543 elected and 2 nominated members.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 12 to 35 of the Indian Constitution collectively deal with Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Justice of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Justice of India.

questions.question: India’s GDP is classified as which type of economy?
questions.explanation: India has a mixed economy with both private and public sector participation.

questions.question: The Union Budget of India is presented by the:
questions.explanation: The Finance Minister presents the Union Budget in Parliament.

questions.question: Which sector contributes the most to India's GDP?
questions.explanation: The services sector contributes the largest share to India’s GDP.

questions.question: The 'Make in India' initiative was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: Make in India was launched in 2014 to promote manufacturing.

questions.question: Which of the following is a noble gas?
questions.explanation: Argon is a noble gas belonging to Group 18 of the periodic table.

questions.question: The process of photosynthesis primarily occurs in which part of the plant?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis mainly occurs in the leaves, which contain chlorophyll.

questions.question: Which technology is used in satellites for communication?
questions.explanation: Satellites use Radio Frequency (RF) technology for communication.

questions.question: The unit of electric resistance is called:
questions.explanation: Ohm (Ω) is the SI unit of electrical resistance.

questions.question: Which of the following is a biodiversity hotspot in India?
questions.explanation: Western Ghats is a recognized biodiversity hotspot in India.

questions.question: The Convention on Biological Diversity was signed in which year?
questions.explanation: The Convention on Biological Diversity was signed at the Earth Summit in Rio de Janeiro in 1992.

questions.question: Which protected area is famous for the Bengal tiger?
questions.explanation: The Sundarbans is famous for its Bengal tigers.

questions.question: Which law is primarily responsible for environmental protection in India?
questions.explanation: The Environment Protection Act, 1986, is the overarching legislation for environmental protection.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Minister of Odisha?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current Chief Minister of Odisha since 12 June 2024.

questions.question: Who is the current External Affairs Minister of India?
questions.explanation: S. Jaishankar is the current External Affairs Minister of India.

questions.question: Which international organization awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2022?
questions.explanation: The World Food Programme received the Nobel Peace Prize in 2022.

questions.question: Which scheme was launched by the Government of India to promote digital payments?
questions.explanation: Cashless India promotes digital transactions and cashless payments.

questions.question: Which sports event did India co-host in 2023?
questions.explanation: India co-hosted the Asian Games in 2023.

questions.question: Who is the current Vice President of India?
questions.explanation: C. P. Radhakrishnan has been the Vice President of India since 12 September 2025.
